title: Parity certificate (Phase 3a)
description: What a parity certificate MUST pin. This is the UNION of the internal storage `certificates` record shape and the richer design task (canon/idmodel versions, projection-algo version, per-dim results). The certificate is a DIGEST-BEARING record: its top-level `digest` field is computed with the canon `certificate` profile (`spec-canon:v2:certificate\n`) and is EXCLUDED from its own preimage (canon sec 8). PRODUCE ONCE, STORE VERBATIM, never recompute (canon sec 9). NO JSON FLOATS. This schema is co-designed with the consumer-integration phase; fields here that consumers also need (digest byte-match domain, parity-gate vs policy-gate) are flagged OPEN, see SEMANTICS.md sec 9.

projection_algo_version.description: Version of the P/N projection+parity ALGORITHM that produced this result. Pinning this makes a certificate reproducible: two certificates over the same inputs but different algo versions are legitimately allowed to differ. (Task item 8.)

canon_version.description: canon contract version used (`v2` since NFCBOUNDARY: NFC at the ingest boundary, digest prefix `spec-canon:v2:`). v1 certificates stay valid as v1 records; a v1 digest is never compared to a v2 digest and a mixed-canon_version graph is rejected.

kind_alignment_version.description: Version of kind-alignment.json used. (The matrix is part of the parity semantics; a matrix change can change results, so it is pinned.)

permitted_freedom_vocab_version.description: Version of spec-parity/permitted-freedom-vocab.json used. The vocabulary defines which permitted_freedom tokens (and thus which below-frontier deviations) the soundness matcher excuses; a vocab change can change the result, so it is pinned (SEMANTICS.md sec 3 / sec 9).

ec_revision_id.description: The REALIZED-side revision_id this certificate is about (the storage `revisions.revision_id` for E(C)). Distinct from the spec/desired revision, which is pinned by spec_revision_digest. revision_alignment (sec 7.5) relates this E(C) revision to the spec revision.

spec_revision_digest.description: Digest pinning the exact spec/desired-graph REVISION (the canon digest of the desired graph's revision record). Distinct from desired_graph_digest: this pins the revision identity, that pins the graph content.

desired_graph_digest.description: canon semantic-content digest of the desired-state graph S that was checked.

ec_digest.description: canon semantic-content digest of E(C) -- the realized Interface Boundary IR extract that was checked. For fixture runs this is the fixture's digest. PARITY OVER A NON-REPRODUCIBLE E(C) IS MEANINGLESS (Benchmark C): a certificate over an E(C) that is not byte-reproducible is advisory only and MUST set ec_reproducible=false.

ec_reproducible.description: true iff E(C) is byte-reproducible (Benchmark C green / fixture). When false the certificate is ADVISORY and downstream gates MUST NOT treat it as authoritative.

code_head_sha.description: The code repo HEAD sha the E(C) was extracted from (provenance pin).

overall_result_state.description: Aggregated state per SEMANTICS.md sec 6.4. Anything other than `pass` (and `not_applicable` which is neutral) makes the certificate NON-GREEN. INVARIANT: byte-equal to the referenced finding_set.overall_result_state (the certificate copy is derived from, never recomputed against, the finding_set at findings_ref).

dimension_results.description: Per-dimension results embedded for at-a-glance gating; the full findings live behind findings_ref. INVARIANT: byte-equal (after canon canonicalization) to the referenced finding_set.dimension_results.

findings_ref.description: Digest ref to the finding_set artifact (result-state.schema.json finding_set). Pointer, not payload (portal metadata_only).

waivers_ref.description: Optional digest ref to the set of waivers in effect for this run. Waived findings remain non-pass in dimension_results; this ref records WHY they are not gating. Absent if no waivers.

digest.description: The AUTHORITATIVE identity of the certificate: the certificate-profile content digest = lowercase hex SHA-256 over `spec-canon:v2:certificate\n` || canonical_bytes(certificate-without-this-top-level-`digest`-field). EXCLUDED from its own preimage (canon sec 8). There is deliberately NO separate hashed `certificate_id` field (that would be circular: it would sit inside the preimage). A `certificate_id` mirror, if a storage index needs one, lives in the non-hashed `locator` (below) and equals this `digest`.

vacuity.description: OPTIONAL, HASHED (inside the certificate digest preimage). Present (is_vacuous=true) ONLY when the certify VACUITY TRIPWIRE fired: E(C) had NO kind-aligned realized boundary (no real code to certify), so overall_result_state is not_applicable across every dimension. This is what lets a consumer tell a vacuous refusal from an ordinary single-revision not_applicable WITHOUT trusting the unhashed finding_set — it is NOT a fake-green. Absent on every non-vacuous certificate (so existing certificates are byte-identical and their digests unchanged). Mirrors finding_set.vacuity.

locator.description: NON-HASHED envelope (canon sec 10): wall-clock created time, producer host/version display, run_id, and an optional `certificate_id` mirror of `digest`. Changing these MUST NOT change `digest`. The hashed `run` digest (if a run is associated) lives in the runs record, not here.

certificate_id.description: Optional storage-index mirror of the top-level `digest`. Non-hashed; equals `digest`. Avoids the circularity of a hashed id field.
